import ThemeStudio from '../../../components/ThemeStudio.tsx';

<p class="kc-lede">Edit every `--kc-color-*` token and the corner radius, light and dark independently, and watch real `kc-*` components reskin as you go. Start from a preset, fine-tune, then copy the CSS — or paste an existing theme back in.</p>

<ThemeStudio client:only="solid" />

## Shipping a theme

This is the production mechanism. The editor writes `--kc-color-*` and `--kc-radius` as inherited custom properties; the kit reads them through the Shadow DOM. **Copy CSS** gives you a paste-ready block:

```css
:root {
  --kc-color-primary: #7c3aed;
  --kc-color-ring: #7c3aed;
  --kc-radius: 0.6rem;
  /* …the full light set */
}

.dark {
  --kc-color-primary: #a78bfa;
  /* …the dark overrides */
}
```

Drop that on `:root` to rebrand every `kc-*` element on the page, or scope it to a wrapper to theme one section. The `--radius-sm…xl` scale derives from `--kc-radius` automatically. See the [theming guide](/chat/guides/theming/) for scoped subtrees and the full token reference.

<p>Theming here is token-only: colors and radius. Type scale lives in the <code>--kc-text-*</code> tokens (covered in the guide). Effects the tokens don't model yet — gradient fills, glow rings, per-bubble surfaces, custom fonts — aren't editable because the kit doesn't expose them as tokens.</p>
